items.title: Arteta verwacht reactie van Arsenal na fluitconcerten
items.summary: Mikel Arteta verwacht een reactie van zijn spelers na de nederlaag tegen Manchester United. Arsenal werd zondag uitgejouwd door de eigen fans na de eerste verliespartij in eigen huis in de Premier League. Met een voorsprong van vier punten liggen The Gunners echter nog steeds op titelkoers.

items.title: Weer complimenten in Italië voor 'buitengewone' Malen
items.summary: Hoewel Donyell Malen veel kansen kreeg om te scoren tegen AC Milan, vond hij het net niet. Toch was AS Roma-trainer Gian Piero Gasperini lovend over de Nederlander.

items.title: 'Sergio Ramos bereikt akkoord en wordt nieuwe eigenaar Sevilla'
items.summary: Groot nieuws uit Spanje: Sergio Ramos heeft een akkoord bereikt met de grootaandeelhouder van Sevilla, waardoor hij de club in handen gaat krijgen. Dat diende zich rond nieuwjaar al aan, maar is nu een nieuwe fase ingegaan.

items.title: Australian Open LIVE! Defending champion Sinner in fourth-round action
Cruciale week Feyenoord: is het 'spinnenweb' van Van Persie sterk genoeg?
items.summary: Voor een club in nood is Heracles Almelo een prima medicijn. Feyenoord won zondag van de degradatiekandidaat, al ging het niet vanzelf, maar boekte zo zijn tweede zege in vier dagen en zag nieuwkomer Mats Deijl debuteren. Komt de ‘frisse, nieuwe start’ waar trainer Robin van Persie over spreekt op tijd om de week van de waarheid te kunnen overleven?

items.title: De harde lessen van Ten Hag voor Van Leer: 'Belde me dat dat écht niet kon'
items.summary: Erik ten Hag hecht veel waarde aan hiërarchie. Dat heeft Benjamin van Leer tijdens zijn loopbaan aan den lijve ondervonden. De oud-doelman is meermaals op zijn plek gezet door Ten Hag, maar kon dat ook wel waarderen.

items.title: Martinez edging closer to Villa career crossroads
items.summary: Emiliano Martinez is hailed as the world's best goalkeeper at Aston Villa home games - but even though his team are riding high, the Argentina international is at a crossroads.

items.title: Pollock: If I was a fan I'd boo me - but I can be England leader soon
items.summary: Henry Pollock has admitted to Sky Sports that "if I was a fan I'd boo me" but has ambitions to move into a "leadership role" with England within the next two years.

items.title: Pegula dethrones Keys to reach Australian Open quarter-finals
items.summary: Jessica Pegula knocked out defending champion Madison Keys at the Australian Open ‌on Monday as the American sixth seed secured a dominant 6-3 6-4 victory to reach the quarter-finals at Melbourne Park for the fourth time.

items.title: Ambiente Friendly looks to thrive over hurdles at Plumpton
items.summary: Ambiente Friendly - who was once second in the Derby - looks to progress his hurdling career at Plumpton on Monday afternoon, live on Sky Sports Racing.

items.title: Beating Arsenal puts Man United into Premier League title race
items.summary: Man United are 12 points behind league-leading Arsenal, but after beating the Gunners on Sunday, can the Red Devils pull off an unlikely title push?

items.title: Scheffler claims 20th PGA Tour title to match feats of Woods and Nicklaus
items.summary: World No 1 Scottie Scheffler secured his 20th PGA Tour title with a four-shot victory at The American Express on his first start of the 2026 season.

items.title: Arteta challenges 'poor' Arsenal after Man Utd loss
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has challenged Arsenal to show the mental strength to win the Premier League after falling to a nervous 3-2 home defeat against Manchester United.

items.title: Oviedo coach: Yamal goal from 'another galaxy'
items.summary: Real Oviedo coach Guillermo Almada branded Lamine Yamal a player from "another galaxy" after the Barcelona forward's wonder goal in Sunday's LaLiga meeting between the two sides.

items.title: Juve's Spalletti sees McKennie as 'perfect' striker
items.summary: Juventus coach Luciano Spalletti advocated for United States midfielder Weston McKennie to play in a more offensive position after his team beat Napoli 3-0 on Sunday.

items.title: Carrick cool after Arsenal win: Big games coming
items.summary: Michael Carrick said Manchester United have "bigger games to come" after a 3-2 victory against Premier League leaders Arsenal.

items.title: 'Another unbelievable result' - but what's different about Man Utd now?
items.summary: MOTD pundit Danny Murphy explains how Manchester United earned their impressive win at Premier League leaders Arsenal, and how he feels the Gunners will react.

items.title: Hardly anyone would have picked Carrick to be Man Utd boss - but now?
items.summary: As Michael Carrick's perfect start at Manchester United continues, is he building a case for the job longer term?

items.title: Why Arsenal's wobble may not even matter - despite 'feeling pressure'
items.summary: Arsenal's winless run may normally be cause for concern - but the stats suggest they are still in control of the Premier League title race.

items.title: Lucas Paquetá edges towards West Ham exit after £35.8m Flamengo bid
items.summary: Brazilian club make improved offer for 28-year-old Sale would allow further transfer spending Flamengo are locked in negotiations with West Ham after making a new £35.8m bid for Lucas Paquetá. Although the offer has not been accepted there is growing optimism on Flamengo’s side that they will secure the Brazil international. The clubs are focused on the payment structure of the deal and it is understood that it is moving in the right direction. Continue reading...
